tactical_shape: Koubek left out Patrik Schick and Tomas Soucek and then seemed intent on staying in the game as long as possible
The side were largely passive and were broken open by Mexico's full-backs rather than imposing a clear attacking shape
standout_players: Patrik Schick was omitted from the starting line-up
Tomas Soucek came on but left the field in obvious distress after landing awkwardly
fatigue_injuries: Tomas Soucek landed awkwardly and left the field in obvious distress
home_read.momentum: Czechia depart the tournament without leaving a trace, having failed to put up anything resembling a fight.

tactical_shape: Javier Aguirre made five changes but kept the team competitive rather than heavily rotated
Mexico attacked through full-backs, with Mateo Chávez and Jorge Sánchez both surging forward from wide areas
Gilberto Mora controlled the game from midfield and helped open Czechia up
standout_players: Gilberto Mora impressed throughout and was involved in the first two Mexico goals
Mateo Chávez scored the opener with a calm finish
Jorge Sánchez drove through to force the second goal, which Julián Quiñones finished
Julián Quiñones tucked in his second of the tournament
Álvaro Fidalgo slammed in the third in injury time
Guillermo Ochoa's introduction was met with a standing ovation; Aguirre called it 'the night of Memo Ochoa'
away_read.momentum: Mexico are on a strong surge, winning three straight without conceding and marching on after topping the group.
